enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Makka Kalanguthappa?

The song conveys a sense of joy and celebration, encouraging listeners to embrace happiness and togetherness.

What is the context of the song in the movie Dharmadurai?

In Dharmadurai, this song highlights moments of festivity and camaraderie among the characters, enhancing the overall theme of the film.

Who is the singer of Makka Kalanguthappa?

The song is sung by Mathichiyam Bala, known for his vibrant and energetic vocal style.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for Makka Kalanguthappa was composed by Yuvan Shankar Raja, a prominent figure in Tamil cinema music.

When was the song released?

The song was released on August 4, 2016.
enrichedMetadata.summary: Makka Kalanguthappa is a lively song that encapsulates the essence of joy and celebration in the film Dharmadurai. The upbeat tempo and engaging lyrics invite listeners to partake in the festivities, making it a perfect anthem for weddings and parties. The song evokes feelings of happiness and togetherness, resonating with the audience's desire for connection and celebration. Mathichiyam Bala's energetic rendition adds to the song's charm, making it a memorable piece in the Tamil music landscape.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Makka Kalanguthappa from Dharmadurai is an energetic anthem that captures the spirit of celebration and joy. Sung by the talented Mathichiyam Bala, this song is sure to uplift your mood and get you dancing. With its catchy lyrics and vibrant beats, it's a perfect addition to any festive playlist.
stanzaMeanings: Oh God, in your temple, is there a lamp?
Oh leader, in your feet, let the light of faith shine.
Children, wake up! Children, wake up! Hold on tight and get ready!
The country is waking up! The people of the country are rising up! What a great king you are!
Children, wake up! Hold on tight and get ready! Children, wake up! Hold on tight and get ready!
The country is waking up! The people of the country are rising up! What a great king you are!
What a great king you are! This king protects this town.
With a garland of rose flowers, with a garland of rose flowers, you are staying strong.
It is a great feeling to be here, oh king!
Here, it is a great feeling, oh king! This town is protected by the king.
When I see you, your green face shines, your face is beautiful like a green fruit. Oh king, you left our world for another world. Why did you go to another world, oh king?
Children, wake up! Hold on tight and get ready!
The country is waking up! The people of the country are rising up! What a great king you are!
